title: WCAG 2.2 Level AA base checkpoints
description: All active WCAG 2.2 Level A and Level AA success criteria required for WCAG 2.2 AA conformance. 4.1.1 Parsing is not active in WCAG 2.2 and is included separately as a legacy helper.

checkpoints.title: Non-text Content
checkpoints.reviewFocus: Images, icons, charts, CAPTCHA, controls, decorative assets
checkpoints.question: Does every meaningful non-text item have an equivalent text alternative, and are purely decorative items ignored by assistive technology?
checkpoints.evidence: Check sampled images/icons/charts/controls. Record examples where alt text is missing, misleading, duplicated, or only a filename.
typo3Hints: Check FAL image metadata, Fluid <f:image> alt handling, CKEditor content images.

checkpoints.title: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
checkpoints.question: Is an equivalent transcript or media alternative available for prerecorded audio-only or video-only content?
checkpoints.evidence: List media URLs and whether transcript/alternative is present.
typo3Hints: Inventory embedded video/audio content elements and third-party iframes.

checkpoints.title: Captions (Prerecorded)
checkpoints.question: Do prerecorded videos with audio provide accurate captions for speech and important sounds?
checkpoints.evidence: Check representative videos and note caption availability/quality.
typo3Hints: Inventory embedded video/audio content elements and third-party iframes.

checkpoints.title: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
checkpoints.question: Is visual information needed to understand prerecorded video available through audio description or a text/media alternative?
checkpoints.evidence: Record videos where key visual information is not available non-visually.
typo3Hints: Inventory embedded video/audio content elements and third-party iframes.

checkpoints.title: Captions (Live)
checkpoints.question: Are captions provided for live audio in synchronized media when live media is offered?
checkpoints.evidence: List live-stream formats and caption provision.
typo3Hints: Inventory embedded video/audio content elements and third-party iframes.

checkpoints.title: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
checkpoints.question: Do prerecorded videos provide audio description when important visual information is not already in the audio track?
checkpoints.evidence: Check videos with visual-only meaning; record missing audio-description alternatives.
typo3Hints: Inventory embedded video/audio content elements and third-party iframes.

checkpoints.title: Info and Relationships
checkpoints.reviewFocus: Headings, lists, tables, forms, layout, semantic relationships
checkpoints.question: Are structure, relationships, labels, table headers, lists and grouping conveyed programmatically and not only visually?
checkpoints.evidence: Inspect semantic markup and accessibility tree for representative content and components.
typo3Hints: Check Fluid partials, custom content elements, navigation partials, accordion/tab JS.

checkpoints.title: Meaningful Sequence
checkpoints.question: When content order matters, does the programmatic/reading order preserve the intended meaning?
checkpoints.evidence: Compare visual order, DOM/accessibility order and keyboard reading order.

checkpoints.title: Sensory Characteristics
checkpoints.question: Do instructions avoid relying only on shape, size, visual position, orientation or sound?
checkpoints.evidence: Record instructions such as 'click the red button on the right' without an accessible textual label.

checkpoints.title: Orientation
checkpoints.question: Does the content work in both portrait and landscape unless a specific orientation is essential?
checkpoints.evidence: Test representative pages/components in both orientations or emulated viewports.

checkpoints.title: Identify Input Purpose
checkpoints.question: Can common user-information inputs be programmatically identified, for example with correct autocomplete tokens?
checkpoints.evidence: Check name, email, address, phone and similar fields.

checkpoints.title: Use of Color
checkpoints.question: Is color never the only way to convey information, identify an action, show state, or distinguish visual elements?
checkpoints.evidence: Check links in text, chart legends, form errors and selected states.

checkpoints.title: Audio Control
checkpoints.question: If audio starts automatically and lasts more than a short moment, can users pause/stop it or control its volume independently?
checkpoints.evidence: Record autoplay audio/video behavior and controls.

checkpoints.title: Contrast (Minimum)
checkpoints.question: Does text meet minimum contrast requirements against its background, including states and overlays?
checkpoints.evidence: Use automated contrast output and manually verify gradients/images/hover/focus states.
typo3Hints: Check SCSS/design tokens, Bootstrap overrides, sticky header/footer, cookie banner.

checkpoints.title: Resize Text
checkpoints.question: Can text be resized to 200% without loss of content or functionality?
checkpoints.evidence: Test representative pages at 200% zoom; record clipping, overlap or inaccessible controls.

checkpoints.title: Images of Text
checkpoints.question: Is real text used instead of images of text, except where the visual presentation is essential or customizable?
checkpoints.evidence: List banners/cards/images where text is embedded in pixels.
typo3Hints: Check FAL image metadata, Fluid <f:image> alt handling, CKEditor content images.

checkpoints.title: Reflow
checkpoints.question: Can content reflow in a narrow viewport without two-dimensional scrolling, except for content where two-dimensional layout is essential?
checkpoints.evidence: Test core pages at 320 CSS px; record horizontal scroll and clipped content.

checkpoints.title: Non-text Contrast
checkpoints.question: Do visual boundaries and state indicators for controls, icons and meaningful graphics have sufficient contrast?
checkpoints.evidence: Check buttons, inputs, focus outlines, chart lines, selected states and icon-only controls.
typo3Hints: Check SCSS/design tokens, Bootstrap overrides, sticky header/footer, cookie banner.

checkpoints.title: Text Spacing
checkpoints.question: Does content remain usable when line, paragraph, letter and word spacing are increased?
checkpoints.evidence: Apply text-spacing bookmarklet/CSS and record clipped/overlapping text or lost controls.

checkpoints.title: Content on Hover or Focus
checkpoints.question: Is additional content triggered by hover/focus dismissible, hoverable and persistent while needed?
checkpoints.evidence: Test tooltips/dropdowns with mouse and keyboard; record failures.

checkpoints.title: Keyboard
checkpoints.question: Can all functionality be operated with a keyboard without requiring pointer gestures?
checkpoints.evidence: Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Space/Escape through representative flows and record blockers.

checkpoints.title: No Keyboard Trap
checkpoints.question: Can keyboard focus always move away from each component using standard or clearly documented keys?
checkpoints.evidence: Check modal, menu, iframe, map, video and third-party embeds.

checkpoints.title: Character Key Shortcuts
checkpoints.question: Can single-character shortcuts be turned off, remapped or active only when the relevant control has focus?
checkpoints.evidence: Ask product owners/devs and test custom widgets/search/gallery shortcuts.

checkpoints.title: Timing Adjustable
checkpoints.question: Can users turn off, adjust or extend time limits unless a documented exception applies?
checkpoints.evidence: Record session timeouts, quiz/booking timers and warning/extension behavior.

checkpoints.title: Pause, Stop, Hide
checkpoints.question: Can users pause, stop or hide moving, blinking, scrolling or auto-updating content that starts automatically and lasts long enough to distract?
checkpoints.evidence: Check sliders, tickers, videos, loaders and live update regions.

checkpoints.title: Three Flashes or Below Threshold
checkpoints.question: Does content avoid flashes above the permitted threshold?
checkpoints.evidence: Review animations/video; use specialist tool for suspect flashing content.

checkpoints.title: Bypass Blocks
checkpoints.question: Can users bypass repeated blocks such as header/navigation and go directly to main content?
checkpoints.evidence: Test skip link visibility/function and landmark structure.

checkpoints.title: Page Titled
checkpoints.question: Does each page have a unique and descriptive title?
checkpoints.evidence: Check title across representative pages, especially search/results/errors/detail pages.

checkpoints.title: Focus Order
checkpoints.question: Does keyboard focus move in an order that preserves meaning and operability?
checkpoints.evidence: Use focus recorder; record surprising jumps, hidden focus, skipped controls or modal leaks.

checkpoints.title: Link Purpose (In Context)
checkpoints.question: Can the purpose of each link be determined from its link text or surrounding context?
checkpoints.evidence: Record generic/ambiguous links like 'more', 'here', icon-only links without accessible names.

checkpoints.title: Multiple Ways
checkpoints.question: Is there more than one way to locate pages within a set, except where the page is a process step or exception applies?
checkpoints.evidence: Check navigation, search, sitemap, related links or breadcrumbs.

checkpoints.title: Headings and Labels
checkpoints.question: Do headings and labels describe the topic or purpose clearly?
checkpoints.evidence: Review headings/labels for clarity, not just presence.
typo3Hints: Check Fluid partials, custom content elements, navigation partials, accordion/tab JS.

checkpoints.title: Focus Visible
checkpoints.question: Is keyboard focus visible on every focusable component and in every state?
checkpoints.evidence: Tab through desktop/mobile layouts and record invisible/low-contrast focus.
typo3Hints: Check SCSS/design tokens, Bootstrap overrides, sticky header/footer, cookie banner.

checkpoints.title: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
checkpoints.question: When a component receives keyboard focus, is it not completely hidden by author-created content?
checkpoints.evidence: Tab with sticky headers, banners and open overlays; record obscured focus targets.
typo3Hints: Check SCSS/design tokens, Bootstrap overrides, sticky header/footer, cookie banner.

checkpoints.title: Pointer Gestures
checkpoints.question: Can functionality using multipoint or path-based gestures also be operated with a single pointer without path gestures, unless essential?
checkpoints.evidence: Test pinch, swipe, drag path and map/gallery controls for simple alternatives.

checkpoints.title: Pointer Cancellation
checkpoints.question: Do pointer actions avoid triggering irreversible actions on down-event, or provide abort/undo/up-event behavior?
checkpoints.evidence: Test mouse/touch down, move away and release; record accidental activation risks.

checkpoints.title: Label in Name
checkpoints.question: Does the accessible name contain the visible label text so speech-input users can activate controls by name?
checkpoints.evidence: Compare visible label with aria-label/labelledby/name for controls.

checkpoints.title: Motion Actuation
checkpoints.question: Can motion-operated functionality also be operated by UI controls, and can accidental motion activation be disabled?
checkpoints.evidence: Check mobile/device features and alternatives.

checkpoints.title: Dragging Movements
checkpoints.question: Can dragging functionality also be achieved by a single pointer without dragging, unless dragging is essential?
checkpoints.evidence: Test sliders, carousels, sortables, maps and upload drop zones for button/click alternatives.

checkpoints.title: Target Size (Minimum)
checkpoints.question: Are pointer targets at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels or covered by an allowed exception/spacing rule?
checkpoints.evidence: Use helper scan for small targets; manually verify exceptions and spacing.
typo3Hints: Check SCSS/design tokens, Bootstrap overrides, sticky header/footer, cookie banner.

checkpoints.title: Language of Page
checkpoints.question: Is the default human language of each page programmatically identified?
checkpoints.evidence: Check html lang for each language/site variant.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 site language configuration and Fluid lang output.

checkpoints.title: Language of Parts
checkpoints.question: Are passages or phrases in a different language identified where pronunciation/meaning would otherwise be wrong?
checkpoints.evidence: Review mixed-language content, navigation labels, quotes and imported content.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 site language configuration and Fluid lang output.

checkpoints.title: On Focus
checkpoints.question: Does receiving focus avoid unexpected context changes?
checkpoints.evidence: Tab through controls and record automatic navigation, submission or layout context changes.

checkpoints.title: On Input
checkpoints.question: Does changing a setting/form control avoid unexpected context changes unless the user is warned beforehand?
checkpoints.evidence: Test selects, filters, checkboxes and autocomplete controls.

checkpoints.title: Consistent Navigation
checkpoints.question: Are repeated navigation mechanisms presented in a consistent relative order across pages?
checkpoints.evidence: Compare homepage, content, listing, detail and form pages.

checkpoints.title: Consistent Identification
checkpoints.question: Are components with the same functionality identified consistently across the site?
checkpoints.evidence: Compare search, login, menu, download, filter and sharing controls.

checkpoints.title: Consistent Help
checkpoints.question: If help mechanisms are provided on multiple pages, are they offered in the same relative order unless the user changes them?
checkpoints.evidence: Check contact/help/chat/FAQ/self-help placement across representative pages.

checkpoints.title: Error Identification
checkpoints.question: Are input errors identified and described to the user in text?
checkpoints.evidence: Submit invalid forms; record whether errors are textual, linked and specific.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 form definitions, ext:form templates, login/search/newsletter forms.

checkpoints.title: Labels or Instructions
checkpoints.question: Are labels or instructions provided when user input is required?
checkpoints.evidence: Check visible labels, required indicators, format hints and placeholder-only fields.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 form definitions, ext:form templates, login/search/newsletter forms.

checkpoints.title: Error Suggestion
checkpoints.question: When an input error is detected and suggestions are known, are correction suggestions provided unless this would compromise security or purpose?
checkpoints.evidence: Submit invalid forms and record helpfulness of correction suggestions.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 form definitions, ext:form templates, login/search/newsletter forms.

checkpoints.title: Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)
checkpoints.question: For important submissions, is there reversibility, validation/correction, or review/confirmation before final submission?
checkpoints.evidence: Check checkout, account changes, financial/legal/data deletion/update flows.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 form definitions, ext:form templates, login/search/newsletter forms.

checkpoints.title: Redundant Entry
checkpoints.question: Are users not forced to re-enter information already provided in the same process unless an exception applies?
checkpoints.evidence: Test multi-step forms, checkout, account setup and application processes.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 form definitions, ext:form templates, login/search/newsletter forms.

checkpoints.title: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
checkpoints.question: Can users authenticate without solving cognitive function tests, or is an accessible alternative/support mechanism available?
checkpoints.evidence: Review login/MFA/captcha/password flows for copy-paste, password managers, alternatives and object-recognition/puzzle CAPTCHAs.
typo3Hints: Check TYPO3 form definitions, ext:form templates, login/search/newsletter forms.

checkpoints.title: Name, Role, Value
checkpoints.question: Do custom and native UI components expose correct name, role, value/state and state changes to assistive technologies?
checkpoints.evidence: Inspect accessibility tree for menu, accordion, tabs, modal, form controls and icon buttons.
typo3Hints: Check Fluid partials, custom content elements, navigation partials, accordion/tab JS.

checkpoints.title: Status Messages
checkpoints.question: Can status messages be programmatically determined without moving focus unexpectedly?
checkpoints.evidence: Test form errors, success messages, live search/filter results, cart/status updates and toasts.

legacyCheckpoints.title: Parsing
legacyCheckpoints.question: Only check this when a contract/legal profile still references WCAG 2.0 or 2.1. WCAG 2.2 removed this criterion as obsolete.
legacyCheckpoints.evidence: Run HTML validation only for legacy reporting if needed.
typo3Hints: Legacy-only. Use HTML validation if a WCAG 2.0/2.1 policy requires it.
